evals.name: tech-article-published
evals.prompt: I want to write a new blog post about how I set up a CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions for a Python project. The post is called 'Automating Python Deployments with GitHub Actions' and the idea is to show the full workflow from commit to production. It's ready to publish, not a draft.
evals.expected_output: A published post markdown file in src/posts/ with frontmatter containing title, date, description, tags (including Python, CI/CD, DevOps), and draft: false. Body is empty.

evals.name: personal-draft
evals.prompt: I have an idea for a draft about why I think Rust is overhyped for web development. Title is 'Rust for the Web - Overkill or Overrated?'. It's just a draft for now, I want to work on it later.
evals.expected_output: A draft file in drafts/rust-for-the-web-overkill-or-overrated/index.md with slug field, ISO date, description, no draft field, no tags.

evals.name: with-image
evals.prompt: Write a new published post called 'My Trip to Japan' about traveling through Tokyo and Kyoto. The description is about the tech scene and temples. I have an image at /images/japan-trip.jpg and the alt text is 'Tokyo skyline at twilight'.
evals.expected_output: A published post in src/posts/ with image and imageDescription fields. Tags should include travel or similar deduced topics. draft: false.
